vocabulary.meaning: to go out (e.g. on an excursion or outing)
meaning_full: to go out (e.g. on an excursion or outing)

example_sentences.english: I often catch cold in winter.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はよく風邪をひく。

example_sentences.japanese: 私はよく映画を見に行く。

example_sentences.japanese: 私はそれについてはよく知らない。
example_sentences.english: I am not certain about that.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はこの問題をよく知っている。
example_sentences.english: I am familiar with this subject.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はこの問題についてよく知っている。
example_sentences.english: I am familiar with this subject.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はこのへんはよく知りません。

example_sentences.japanese: この歌はよく知ってるよ。
example_sentences.english: This song is familiar to me.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はよく風邪を引く。
example_sentences.english: I often catch cold.

example_sentences.japanese: 両親がよろしくと言ってました。
example_sentences.english: My parents send you their best regards.

example_sentences.japanese: 妹さんによろしくね。
example_sentences.english: Say hello to your sister for me.

example_sentences.japanese: お姉さんによろしくね。
example_sentences.english: Say hello to your sister for me.

example_sentences.japanese: どうぞよろしく。
example_sentences.english: Pleased to meet you.

example_sentences.japanese: ご両親によろしく。
example_sentences.english: Give my best respects to your parents.

example_sentences.japanese: ご家族によろしく。
example_sentences.english: Please remember me to all your family.

example_sentences.japanese: あなたの友だちによろしく。
example_sentences.english: Say hello to your friends.

example_sentences.japanese: 台所をよろしく。
example_sentences.english: You do the kitchen.

example_sentences.japanese: 「よろしくね」「こちらこそ、よろしくね」
example_sentences.english: "Nice to meet you." "Nice to meet you, too."

example_sentences.japanese: よろしくね！
example_sentences.english: Thank you in advance.

example_sentences.japanese: 私の家の近くにりっぱな公園がある。
example_sentences.english: There is a fine park near my house.

example_sentences.japanese: りんごを少し買ってください。
example_sentences.english: Please buy a few apples.

example_sentences.japanese: 誰でも欲しい人にこのりんごをあげよう。
example_sentences.english: I'll give this apple to whoever wants it.

example_sentences.japanese: 果物で一番りんごが好き。
example_sentences.english: I prefer apples to all the other fruits.

example_sentences.japanese: りんごを食べています。
example_sentences.english: I am eating an apple.

example_sentences.japanese: この箱にはりんごが入っている。
example_sentences.english: This box contains apples.

example_sentences.japanese: このりんごは甘い。
example_sentences.english: This apple is sweet.

example_sentences.japanese: このりんごはとても赤い。
example_sentences.english: This apple is very red.

example_sentences.japanese: 私たちは黄色のりんごを持っています。
example_sentences.english: We have yellow apples.

example_sentences.japanese: そのりんごは赤くない。
example_sentences.english: That apple isn't red.

example_sentences.japanese: りんごが嫌いな子どももいる。
example_sentences.english: There are also some children who don't like apples.

part_of_speech: noun or participle which takes the aux. verb suru
nouns which may take the genitive case particle 'no'

example_sentences.japanese: 私は山にいました。

example_sentences.english: I'm undressing.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はアメリカへ行きたい。

example_sentences.japanese: 私はアメリカの料理が大好きです。
example_sentences.english: I'm a big fan of American cooking.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はアメリカの家族のところにいました。

example_sentences.japanese: 私はアメリカに住みたい。

example_sentences.japanese: 私はアメリカで生まれた。
example_sentences.english: I was born in America.

example_sentences.japanese: エレベーターはどこですか。

example_sentences.japanese: エレベーター使おうよ。
example_sentences.english: Let's take the elevator.

example_sentences.japanese: エレベーターはどこですか？
example_sentences.english: Where is the elevator?

example_sentences.japanese: エレベーターが来たよ。
example_sentences.english: The lift arrived.

example_sentences.japanese: エレベーター来たよ。
example_sentences.english: The elevator arrived.

example_sentences.japanese: エレベーター使おう！
example_sentences.english: Let's take the elevator!

example_sentences.japanese: このエレベーターは上に行くよ。
example_sentences.english: This elevator is going up.

example_sentences.english: Did you take the elevator?

example_sentences.english: I can write hiragana, but I can't yet write katakana.

vocabulary.meaning: cup (drinking vessel, measure, brassiere, prize, etc.)
meaning_full: cup (drinking vessel, measure, brassiere, prize, etc.)

example_sentences.japanese: 疲れたから、コーヒーカップに乗ってゆっくりしたいです。
example_sentences.english: I'm tired. I just want to go on the teacup ride and take it easy.

example_sentences.english: There is a cup on the table.

example_sentences.japanese: あなた、私のカップから飲んでますよ。
example_sentences.english: You're drinking out of my cup.

example_sentences.english: Would you like a cupcake?

example_sentences.japanese: カップにはちょっと水が入っている。
example_sentences.english: There is a little water in the cup.

example_sentences.japanese: 私は新しいカメラを買いたい。

example_sentences.japanese: カレンダーはある？

example_sentences.english: I made curry for the first time.

example_sentences.english: This curry is very hot.

example_sentences.english: Today it will also be Japanese curry.

example_sentences.japanese: 昨夜はカレーだった。
example_sentences.english: I had a curry last night.

example_sentences.english: I am the tallest in our class.

example_sentences.japanese: 子供たちはケーキが好きだ。

example_sentences.japanese: もう一つケーキはいかがですか。

example_sentences.japanese: そのケーキが少し欲しい。

example_sentences.japanese: このケーキはおいしいですよ。

example_sentences.english: "Is there a glass?" "Yes, there's one on the table."

example_sentences.japanese: コップが一つ欲しい。

example_sentences.japanese: このコップって、きれい？
example_sentences.english: Is this cup clean?

example_sentences.japanese: 私はあの店で赤いセーターを買った。

example_sentences.japanese: 一の前はゼロです。

example_sentences.japanese: 私は駅までタクシーに乗った。

example_sentences.english: This coffee is not hot enough.

example_sentences.japanese: このコーヒー、ぬるいね。
example_sentences.english: This coffee is not hot enough.

example_sentences.japanese: ありがとう、それだけだよ。
example_sentences.english: Thanks, that's all.

example_sentences.japanese: 電話、どうもありがとう。
example_sentences.english: Thank you for calling.

example_sentences.japanese: 先生、ありがとう。
example_sentences.english: Thank you, Doctor.

example_sentences.japanese: 今日はどうもありがとう。
example_sentences.english: Thank you for today.

example_sentences.japanese: 元気よ、ありがとう。
example_sentences.english: Quite well, thank you.

example_sentences.japanese: 元気です。ありがとう。あなたはどうですか。
example_sentences.english: I'm fine, thank you. And you?

example_sentences.japanese: 楽しい夜をありがとう。
example_sentences.english: Thank you for the pleasant evening.

example_sentences.japanese: きれいな花をありがとう。
example_sentences.english: Thank you for the beautiful flowers.

example_sentences.japanese: お手紙、どうもありがとう。
example_sentences.english: Thank you for writing.

example_sentences.japanese: おいしいコーヒー、どうもありがとう。
example_sentences.english: Thank you very much for the excellent coffee.

example_sentences.japanese: おはよう！どこ行きたい？
example_sentences.english: Good morning! Where do you want to go?

example_sentences.japanese: おはよう。
example_sentences.english: Good morning!

example_sentences.japanese: おはよう。起きる時間だよ。
example_sentences.english: Good morning. It's time to wake up.

example_sentences.japanese: おはよう！
example_sentences.english: Good morning!

meaning_full: to get someone to do something

example_sentences.japanese: ではまた明日。
example_sentences.english: See you again tomorrow.

example_sentences.japanese: ではまた近いうちに！
example_sentences.english: See you soon!

example_sentences.japanese: 犬の方が猫より好きです。
example_sentences.english: I like dogs better than cats.

example_sentences.japanese: 弟は私より背が高い。
example_sentences.english: My younger brother is taller than I.

example_sentences.japanese: 弟はいつも私より早く起きる。
example_sentences.english: My brother always gets up earlier than I.

example_sentences.japanese: 町は村よりも大きい。
example_sentences.english: Towns are larger than villages.

example_sentences.japanese: 遅くても来ないよりまし。
example_sentences.english: Better late than never.

example_sentences.japanese: 誰もいないよりは、誰でもいいからいた方いい。
example_sentences.english: Anybody is better than nobody.

example_sentences.japanese: 秋より春のほうが好きだ。
example_sentences.english: I prefer spring to fall.

example_sentences.japanese: 私は遊んでいるよりは働くほうが好きだ。
example_sentences.english: I prefer working to doing nothing.

example_sentences.japanese: 私は物を書くよりも本を読む方が好きだ。
example_sentences.english: I prefer reading to writing.

example_sentences.japanese: 私は猫より犬の方が好きです。
example_sentences.english: I like dogs more than cats.

example_sentences.japanese: 失礼ですがどなたでしょうか。
example_sentences.english: I am afraid you have the advantage of me.

example_sentences.japanese: 失礼ですが、あなたは英語を話されますか。
example_sentences.english: Excuse me. Do you speak English?

example_sentences.japanese: 失礼しました。
example_sentences.english: Excuse my clumsiness.

example_sentences.japanese: なんて失礼な人でしょう。
example_sentences.english: What a rude man!

example_sentences.japanese: どうも失礼。
example_sentences.english: I'm sorry.

example_sentences.japanese: ではこれで失礼します。
example_sentences.english: I am afraid I must be going now.

example_sentences.japanese: ちょっと失礼します。
example_sentences.english: Excuse me a minute.

example_sentences.japanese: ちょっと失礼。
example_sentences.english: Sorry...

example_sentences.japanese: それじゃお先に失礼します。
example_sentences.english: Then, I'll be leaving you.

example_sentences.japanese: お話中失礼します。
example_sentences.english: I'm sorry to interrupt you.

example_sentences.japanese: 今日は、十五夜です。
example_sentences.english: Today is the night of the full moon.
